ref: As Jesus was on his way to Jerusalem, he took his twelve disciples aside and told them in private:
text: Yesu na wa pãa duoro a Jerusalɛm, wʊ tara na a poturbo pie nɩ ayi yin chɛ̃chɛ̃ɛ na wʊ tɩ 'yɛr kʊ ba,
translated: When Jesus had finished speaking in Jerusalem, he went and sat on the Mount of Olives, and his disciples came to him.

ref: When John the Baptist showed you how to do right, you would not believe him. But these evil people did believe. And even when you saw what they did, you still would not change your minds and believe. Renters of a Vineyard (Mark
text: Jɔɔn Kɔ̃suoro wan wa wile yɩ a yelmãɛ sɔr ka yaa sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ, tɩchɛ a lõpodierbe nɩ a pɔɔ baar sɔɔn de wʊ, yɩn sɔɔ wa nyɛ gba yaa lɩɛb a yɩ tɩɛr sɔɔ de wʊ ɛ.
translated: John Konsuoro said that he went to the farm and when he was coming back, he met a hyena that wanted to eat him, but he ran and climbed a tree and the hyena could not catch him.

ref: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
text: Yɩ vɛ̃ ka a yɩ ɔ̃ɔ ɩ ɔ̃ɔ ka a yɩ kai ɩ kai, bon 'lɔ na gar a nɩtɔ̃ yin a dɛɛr sʊɔ sɛ̃.
translated: If you go and you stay, and you stay and you go, you will not find a place to sit and rest.

ref: The field is the world, and the good seeds are the people who belong to the kingdom. The weeds are those who belong to the evil one,
text: A pʊɔ lɛ ɩ a wɛr. A bʊ̃bivɩɛl lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ bibiir. A mɔɩ lɛ a bala na ɩ a dɛɛr sʊɔ bibiir.
translated: He went to the bush. He met Naaŋmɩn’s children in the bush. He took them and came home with the two children.

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: A bala na ba puor ɛ, lɛ de a ba tɩɛr 'yɔ̃ɔ a anyãna pɔ, a yɩ Sãa bɔ̃ɔ naa ɩka yɩ bʊɔrɔ naa a bonsɩ anyãna a jaa.
translated: If a child is not taught, when he grows up and sees people farming, he will say that the grass is what is being farmed and not the crops.

ref: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
text: Wʊn wa wõ a Yesu yele, wʊ tu naa a puor tɩ sɩɩr a wʊ fuu,
translated: If you believe in Jesus, you will not walk in darkness but in light.

ref: God blesses those people who are merciful. They will be treated with mercy!
text: Pʊpɛl lɛ kʊ yɩn banyã na kaara nɩrɛ nɩbaal, Nãaŋmɩn mɩ na kãan a yɩ nɩbaal.
translated: The world and everything in it belong to the people, but God is the one who created the people.

ref: The woman was Greek and had been born in the part of Syria known as Phoenicia. She begged Jesus to force the demon out of her daughter.
text: A pɔɔ ba dɔ̃ɔ ɩ a Juu nɩrɛ ɛ, na wʊ dɔ̃ɔ yi be a Siriya Fonisiya paal pɔ. Wʊ sʊɔr naa a Yesu ɩka, wʊ dii a sɩdɛɛr bar a wʊ bipɔɔ pɔ.
translated: He went to live in a city called Nazareth, and so the word of the prophets came true: “He will be called a Nazarene.”

ref: “I feel sorry for these people. They have been with me for three days, and they don't have anything to eat.
text: “A nɩbɛ banyã ɩrɛ naa nɩbaal, bojũu ber ata nɩ nyã ban be a n sɛ̃, baa lɛ tara bon na di ɛ.
translated: When the child is born, the mother’s back is strong, but when the child grows up, she carries the burden alone.

ref: But you let people get by without helping their parents when they should. You let them say that what they have has been offered to God.
text: Tɩchɛ yɩn 'yɛr ɩka nɩrɛ 'lɔ jaa na 'yɛr a wʊ sãa bɩɩ ma ɩka, sʊ̃ʊfʊ 'lɔ jaa ɩn tara naa kʊ fʊ, n den kʊ Nãaŋmɩn.
translated: When you see a child crying, it is because of something, and if you do not know what it is, you should ask the child's mother.

ref: Then he said to the servants, “It is time for the wedding banquet, and the invited guests don't deserve to come.
text: “Tɩ na wʊ pãa 'yɛr kʊ a wʊ tʊ̃tʊ̃nbɔ, ‘Yele jaa chɔɔr baar, tɩchɛ a bala ɩn bʊɔl ba sɛɛ ɛ.
translated: If you want to know if someone is a true friend, look at how they behave when you are in trouble.

ref: They said to the soldiers, “Tell everyone that Jesus' disciples came during the night and stole his body while you were asleep.
text: tɩ na ba 'yɛr kʊ ba, “Yɩ 'yɛr ɩka, ‘A wʊ poturbo lɛ wa ju a wʊ ãgãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ sɩn chɛnɛ gã guro.’
translated: They said to their children, “You children should say, ‘We are going to greet our grandfather and then we will return quickly.’

ref: So God's promise came true, just as Isaiah the prophet had said,
text: Wʊ maal a nɩtɔ̃, ka a lɛ a Nãaŋmɩn na dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr tu a 'yɛ'yɛrɛ Isaiya na ɩ yelmãɛ,
translated: He was a prophet, and he wrote what the Lord said to the prophet Isaiah and his message.

ref: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
text: Ba den tãɩtuo 'yɔ̃ɔ dãa pɔ ɩka a Yesu wʊ nyũ, tɩ wʊn wa lɛm kaa, wʊ tɔr bar.
translated: When the time came for Jesus to leave this world, he went to a village.

ref: and he told the crowd to sit down on the grass. Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples, and they gave it to the people.
text: Wʊ vɛ̃n ka a nɩyɔɔ banyãna jɛ̃ tẽe. Wʊ den a paanʊ̃ɔsɩ anũu nɩ a jãmɛ ayi ala dʊl kaa saaju tɩ puor Nãaŋmɩn yãan tɩ na wʊ ŋma de kʊ a poturbo ɩka ba de põ a nɩbɛ. Ka a poturbo de põ a nɩbɛ.
translated: He went and did not find his brothers there. He asked his sisters where his brothers had gone and they told him that they had gone to the farm because their father had sent them to go and clear a new farm. So he went to clear a new farm.

ref: Everyone answered, “We and our own families will take the blame for his death!”
text: Be lɛ ka a nɩbɛ ba jaa 'mataa 'yɛr, “Vɛ̃ ka a wʊ jãɩ dɔɔl a sɩn nɩ a sɩ bibiir ju.”
translated: The man said to his children, “Go and tell your mother that she should come and see something.”

ref: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
text: Ala lɛ so ka dãbãɛ kpɛ ma ka de a fʊ sɛlbir tɩ ũu bar. Nyɛ a fʊ sɛlbir.’
translated: The person who came to the house yesterday is the one who took your bicycle. It is your bicycle.

ref: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
text: A bala na kaara a dor jon kul a tẽe pɔ nɩ a tẽbil ala na gbʊr tɩ 'yɛr a lɛ na ɩ. Ka a nɩbɛ yi chen ɩka ba tɩ kaa.
translated: The child went to the farm and saw that the groundnuts were ripe, so he dug them up and brought them home. Then his mother cooked them and they ate.

ref: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.
text: “A lɛ jũu, nɩrɛ jaa na wõ a n 'yɛrbie anyãna tɩ maala a lɛ na kaara fɩka yɛ̃ sʊɔ na mɛ a wʊ yir dɔɔl pie ju.
translated: When he came, he saw that his younger brother had already gone to the farm and had not yet returned home.

ref: But this is just the beginning of troubles.
text: A dɔɔyɛ anyãna, an kaara fɩka a pɔɔ na ma dɔɔrɔ ka a wõnõ wʊ.
translated: If you love someone, you do not say bad things about them to the people they live with.

ref: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
text: Ba bar naa a nɩyɔyɔɔ bala tɩchɛ ka a wʊ poturbo tara wʊ, ka ba 'matãan a gborsɩ amɩne gɔ̃ɔnɔ̃.
translated: They said that this woman was very beautiful and had a good heart, but her husband's family did not like her.

ref: About nine that morning, the man saw some other people standing in the market with nothing to do.
text: “A mɔ̃tɔ̃ɔ nɩ lɛ wa do bãlãa wʊ yɩn tɩ nyɛ a nɩbɛ bamɩne na ara a daa pɔ na baa maala bon jaa ɛ.
translated: The person who came to the house first is the one who should be given the food before the others.

ref: But when I force out demons by the power of God's Spirit, it proves that God's kingdom has already come to you.
text: Ala ɩ Satan diin Satan kʊ yi na wʊ ŋma dẽdẽme ŋmɛ lɛ a wʊ nãalʊ̃ʊ na ɩ ara? Ala ɩ Nãaŋmɩn Sɩɛ lɛ kʊ ma a kpɛ̃ɔ ka dire nɩ a sɩdɛbɛ ka a yire wiil ɩka a Nãaŋmɩn nãalʊ̃ʊ ta yɩ naa.
translated: Is it Satan himself who is able to make you fall, or is it your own heart? It is the Lord God who gives you strength so that you can stand firm and not fall into what the Lord has forbidden.

ref: Then he went up on a mountain where he could be alone and pray. Later in the evening, he was still there.
text: Wʊn wa wɛl a nɩbɛ baar, wʊ don a tɔ̃ɔ ju ɩka wʊ tɩ sʊɔr Nãaŋmɩn. Jãanʊɔr na wa ta, wʊ chɛnɛ na be a be a wʊ yõ.
translated: When a person dies, they leave everything behind and go to the land of Nãaŋmɩn. No one knows the day, they just go and that is it.

ref: Pilate said to them, “All right, take some of your soldiers and guard the tomb as well as you know how.”
text: Ka a Pilate 'yɛr, “Yɩ de gugurbo ka ba tɩ gu a yaa a lɛ yɩn bɔ̃ɔ.”
translated: Then Pilate said, “I will have him flogged and then release him.”

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Yɩn ba wa vɛ̃ a yɩ taaba yeldɛbɛ kʊ ba ɛ, a yɩ Sãa Nãaŋmɩn mɩ kʊ̃ vɛ̃ a yɩ yeldɛbɛ kʊ yɩ ɛ.
translated: The person who loves you will not tell you lies, and Sãa Nãaŋmɩn also will not tell you lies.

ref: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
text: Baa bɔ̃ɔn ɛ, tɩ tãn a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ a kɔ̃paalaa na wa di ba, lɛ mɩ lɛ an kaara a maa Nɩsaal Bie waa daar.
translated: When the world ends, those who are alive will see their companions die, and I will also go and meet the Lord God.

ref: “Get up! Pick up your mat and go on home.”
text: “Fʊ̃ʊ lɛ n 'yɛrɛ, ir, na fʊ 'mɔɔ a bʊ̃gãnaa, na fʊ kulo nɩ.”
translated: If you have a child, you must raise him well, and you will reap the benefits.

ref: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.

translated: One day, Jesus took Peter, James, and John with him and went up a high mountain.

ref: But the renters grabbed those servants. They beat up one, killed one, and stoned one of them to death.
text: “Tɩchɛ ka a nɩbɛ bala nyɔɔ a wʊ gbãgbaar ŋme a ãsʊɔ, na ba kʊ a ãsʊɔ tɩ na ba lɔb a ãsʊɔ nɩ kʊsɩbɛ kʊ.
translated: The child who does not listen to advice will grow up and face difficulties, and when he faces those difficulties he will remember the advice he was given.

ref: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew
text: A Jɔɔn poturbo na wõ a lɛ, ba chen tɩ 'mɔɔ wʊ na ba ũu.
translated: John was going to the farm when he met his friend on the way.

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Ba wa naa a Yesu sɛ̃ wa nyɛ a daba 'lɔ na dɔ̃ɔ tara a sɩdɛyɔɔ gele ka wʊ chɩ su bonsɩ jãnɛ, ka a dãbãɛ kpɛ ba.
translated: Jesus said to them, "Truly I tell you, in the renewal of all things, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel."

ref: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
text: A lɛ jũu vɛ̃ ka ba gu a yaa tɩ tãn a ber ata daar, ka ala ba ɩ lɛ ɛ, a wʊ poturbo na chen tɩ ju a ãgãn a yaa pɔ, tɩ 'yɛr kʊ a nɩbɛ ɩka, 'lɔ lɛ ir a kũu pɔ, ka a bɛlfʊ taa ɩ dɛɛr gar a lɛ wʊn dɔ̃ɔ 'yɛr ɛ.”
translated: If a person is going to the farm and meets his father’s mother on the way, and she asks him, he must not say he is going to the farm; he must say he is going to look for something to eat, because if he says he is going to the farm, she will forbid him and he will never again go to the farm.

ref: While Peter was sitting out in the courtyard, a servant girl came up to him and said, “You were with Jesus from Galilee.”
text: Pita jãnɛ naa a yõo a bɛchaar pɔ. Ka a gbãgbapɔɔbile ãsʊɔ wa a wʊ sɛ̃ wa 'yɛr, “Fʊ mɩ dɔ̃ɔ turo a Yesu na yi a Galilee.”
translated: Peter went out to the courtyard and wept bitterly. And when the rooster crowed, he remembered the word of the Lord, “Before the rooster crows, you will deny me three times.”

ref: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
text: Ka a Yesu 'yɛr, “A ba sɛɛ ɩka fʊ de bibiir bʊ̃diir lɔb 'yɔ̃ɔ baar ɛ.”
translated: And Jesus said, “I am the bread of life; whoever comes to me will never be hungry.”

ref: But the Son of Man goes around eating and drinking, and you say, “That man eats and drinks too much! He is even a friend of tax collectors and sinners.” Yet Wisdom is shown to be right by what it does. The Unbelieving Towns (Luke
text: Tɩchɛ ka a Nɩsaal Bie wa na wʊ ma dire tɩ nyuro ka ba 'yɛr ɩka di dire nɩ dãburo lɛ wʊ ɩ, na wʊ ɩ a lõpodierbe nɩ a yelbe-ɩrbɛ baraa. A ma wiil a lɛ Nãaŋmɩn na tor, bala wa nyɛ lɛ a wʊ nɩbɛ na kpɩɛrɛ nɩ yɛ̃.”
translated: The reason why God created you is not so that hardship should make you abandon what you are doing and follow a bad path, but so that you should become a useful person who helps others. It is not good for you to sit idle and say that because of poverty you will not work.

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”

translated: The man said to his wife, “If you go to the river to fetch water, don’t go, I will go.”

ref: The disciples talked this over and said to each other, “He must be saying this because we didn't bring along any bread.”
text: Ba piel mana kʊrɔ taa, “Sɩn ba waan paanʊ̃ɔ jaa lɛ so.”
translated: They said to the man, “We will not give you the child again.”

ref: Then Jesus said to the officer, “You may go home now. Your faith has made it happen.” Right then his servant was healed. Jesus Heals Many People (Mark
text: Yesu 'yɛr kʊ a sojasɩ nɩdiere, “Kulo, a ɩ a lɛ kʊ fʊ, fʊn tara a sɔɔfʊ jũu.” A tʊ̃tʊ̃nɔ̃ sa naa a sɔ̃ɔ 'lɔ̃nɔ̃ pɔ.
translated: Jesus said to the soldiers, “Kulo, if you do not leave him alone, you will see trouble.” Then they immediately left him alone.

text: Tɩchɛ wʊ ma bul ɩ tɩkpɛ̃ɛ na wʊ tɛ wilbɛrɛ, ka a lubil ma wa mara a wʊ 'maar pɔ.”
